Fees.Borrow Interest: All interest paid by borrowers from all markets (excluding GHO).

methodology.UserFees: All swap, outbound and affiliate fees paid by users when swapping through THORChain.
methodology.Fees: Slip-based liquidity (swap) fees paid by users on each chain's THORChain pools, the protocol's net outbound (network) fee, and affiliate fees charged by interfaces/wallets (affiliate fees are attributed to the THORChain native chain). RUNE block-reward emissions are excluded.
methodology.Revenue: The 5% of swap fees burned as RUNE (value to all RUNE holders) plus protocol-kept income (net outbound network fee, developer fund and marketing fund). The node-bonder share of swap fees is treated as a security cost and the LP, affiliate and TCY shares as supplier payments, so none of those count as revenue.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Income kept by the protocol: the net outbound network fee plus the 5% developer fund and 5% marketing fund taken from swap fees.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Value to RUNE holders: the 5% of swap fees burned as RUNE (permanently removed from supply), the only component that accrues to every RUNE holder.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Value paid to suppliers: the node-operator (RUNE bonder) share of swap fees set by the Incentive Pendulum (a security cost), the liquidity-provider share of swap fees (LP side of the Incentive Pendulum), affiliate fees passed through to integrators, and the 10% of swap fees paid to TCY stakers.

Fees.Swap Fees: Slip-based liquidity (swap) fees paid by users on each chain's THORChain pools.
Fees.Outbound Fees: Net outbound network fee (outbound gas charged minus gas reimbursed) paid by users.
Fees.Affiliate Fees: Fees charged by the interface or wallet that built the swap (attributed to the THORChain native chain).

UserFees.Swap Fees: Slip-based liquidity (swap) fees paid by users on each chain's THORChain pools.
UserFees.Outbound Fees: Net outbound network fee (outbound gas charged minus gas reimbursed) paid by users.
UserFees.Affiliate Fees: Fees charged by the interface or wallet that built the swap (attributed to the THORChain native chain).

Revenue.RUNE Burn: 5% of swap fees burned, permanently removing RUNE from supply (since 2024-09-16).
Revenue.Outbound Fees To Protocol: Net outbound network fee kept by the protocol.
Revenue.Developer Fund: 5% of swap fees allocated to the developer fund (since 2024-09-16).
Revenue.Marketing Fund: 5% of swap fees allocated to the marketing fund (since 2025-11-04).

ProtocolRevenue.Outbound Fees To Protocol: Net outbound network fee kept by the protocol, a network-level figure split across chains by each chain's share of daily swap volume.
ProtocolRevenue.Developer Fund: 5% of swap fees allocated to the developer fund (since 2024-09-16).
ProtocolRevenue.Marketing Fund: 5% of swap fees allocated to the marketing fund (since 2025-11-04).

HoldersRevenue.RUNE Burn: 5% of swap fees burned, permanently removing RUNE from supply and accruing value to RUNE holders (since 2024-09-16).

SupplySideRevenue.Swap Fees To RUNE Bonders: Node operators' (RUNE bonders') share of swap fees, set network-wide by the Incentive Pendulum - a security cost paid to the nodes that bond RUNE to secure the network.
SupplySideRevenue.Swap Fees To LPs: Liquidity providers' share of swap fees (the LP side of the Incentive Pendulum).
SupplySideRevenue.Affiliate Fees To Integrators: Affiliate fees passed through to the integrator that built the swap.
SupplySideRevenue.TCY Staker Rewards: 10% of swap fees paid in RUNE to TCY stakers (since 2025-05-01).

methodology.Fees: total fees paid by users from Colony DEX activities.
methodology.Revenue: Revenue distributed to token stakers includes 100% from CLY staking and unstaking fees, 50% of CAI fees, 8% of early-stage activity fees (ceTokens), and 70% of Validator Program activity revenues. This distribution creates a strong APY for CLY stakers, incentivizing protocol staking and long-term involvement in Colony's protocol.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Revenue sources directly retained by the protocol include 50% from CAI fees (minting 0.20%, redemption 0.5%, and management 1%), 2% from early-stage platform activities (ceTokens distribution), 2% from USDC fundraised by a project, 16.66% from Colony DEX swap fees, and 30% from Validator Program activities. These funds support the protocol's ongoing development and operations.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: 83.33% of the fees collected from Colony DEX transactions are distributed to liquidity providers. Additionally, 10% of ceTokens are distributed to staked liquidity providers on Colony's DEX, incentivizing them to continue providing liquidity and supporting the exchange. Additional revenue can be generated through farm rewards based on specific marketing campaigns and incentive programs.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Revenue distributed to token stakers includes 100% from CLY staking and unstaking fees, 50% of CAI fees, 8% of early-stage activity fees (ceTokens), and 70% of Validator Program activity revenues. This distribution creates a strong APY for CLY stakers, incentivizing protocol staking and long-term involvement in Colony's protocol.

Fees.bidnames: Action in the eosio.system contract to place a bid on premium (short <12 character) account names via auction.
Fees.ramfee: System account (eosio.ramfee) that collects the 0.5% fee from RAM buy/sell transactions to fund network operations.
Fees.cpuloan: REX-related action (e.g., fundcpuloan or defcpuloan) to manage funding or deferring CPU resource loans from staked tokens for temporary bandwidth boosts.
Fees.netloan: Database table (netloan) in the system contract tracking active REX loans specifically for NET bandwidth resources rented via actions like rentnet.
Fees.powerup: System action to pay a fee (in core tokens) for renting a fractional share of CPU and/or NET resources from the chain-owned pool for a configurable period (typically 24 hours), replacing staking/REX as the primary resource allocation model.

methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Sommelier protocol's share of collected fees after strategist split. Split ratio varies by cellar (typically 50-85% to protocol). These fees are bridged to Sommelier Chain for SOMM staker distribution and token buybacks/burns.

methodology.UserFees:
A minor fee is collected on each swap, functioning as trading fees.
The fees are set at 0.07% on Ethereum and 0.1% on other chains.
On other networks, fees may vary between different pairs and chains.
Refer to https://docs.smardex.io/overview/what-is-smardex/fees for detailed information.
methodology.Fees:
A minor fee is collected on each swap, functioning as trading fees.
The fees are set at 0.07% on Ethereum and 0.1% on other chains.
On other networks, fees may vary between different pairs and chains.

methodology.Revenue: 0.02% of each swap on Ethereum is collected for staking pool (SDEX holders that staked). methodology.UserFees: Borrower / delegate fees flowing into the pools.
methodology.Fees: Interest and delegation rewards paid into the GLIF lending pools by Filecoin Storage Providers (FIL borrow APR) and Impossible Cloud Network node operators (ICN delegation rewards). Computed each day as LP yield from share-rate appreciation of iFIL / stICNT, grossed up by the 10% protocol treasury fee.
methodology.Revenue: 10% protocol fee on both pools. Filecoin: per docs.glif.io/en/for-liquidity-providers/glif-reward-mechanism-ifil iFIL holders earn 90% of SP borrow interest and the protocol treasury takes the remaining 10%, observable on events.glif.link/pool/0/fees. Base: on-chain PoolV2.treasuryFee() returns 0.1e18 = 10%, applied to each ICN node reward claim before LP rewards are restaked.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: 10% protocol fee on both pools. Filecoin: 10% of SP borrow interest goes to the GLIF protocol treasury (events.glif.link/pool/0/fees). Base: 10% of ICN node rewards goes to the protocol treasury (PoolV2.treasuryFee() == 0.1e18).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: LP yield realised through iFIL / stICNT share-rate appreciation. On Base PoolV2.restakePercentage() == 100% so the LP portion of each reward claim is restaked into the pool and lands on totalAssets() for the next convertToAssets query.

Fees.Assets Yields: Total assets yields generated by Filecoin InfinityPoolV2 and Base ICN pools

Revenue.Assets Yields To Protocol: 10% of assets yields goes to the GLIF protocol treasury (events.glif.link/pool/0/fees).

SupplySideRevenue.Assets Yields To LP: 90% of assets yields accrues to iFIL and stICNT holders via share-rate appreciation.

methodology.Fees: Total swap fees paid by traders. Per-pool fee rate read from PoolFactory.getFee (default sAMM 0.05%, vAMM 0.30%; customizable per pool) applied to each swap's input amount.
methodology.Revenue: veAERO holders' share of swap fees, equal to HoldersRevenue (Aerodrome's zero-leak model routes all protocol revenue to voters).
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Percentage of swap fees going to treasury
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Fees earned by LPs staked in the gauge — forwarded to FeeVotingReward for distribution to veAERO voters. Computed per pool as fees × pool.balanceOf(gauge) / pool.totalSupply.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Unstaked LPs' pro-rata share of swap fees, claimable directly from the pool. Computed per pool as fees × (1 − stakedShare). Aerodrome v2 has no unstaked-LP rake module, so unstaked LPs keep 100% of their share.

Fees.Token Swap Fees: All swap fees paid by traders on Aerodrome v2 pools.
Fees.External Bribes Rewards: External bribes deposited to BribeVotingReward contracts (NotifyReward events filtered to the v2 GaugeFactory). Pre-launch tokens are priced via hardcoded conversion rates until each token's cutoff timestamp; afterwards DefiLlama spot pricing is used.

Revenue.Staked-LP Fees And Unstaked-LP Rake: Staked-LP share of swap fees, forwarded to veAERO voters via FeeVotingReward.
Revenue.External Bribes Revenue: External bribes deposited to BribeVotingReward contracts (NotifyReward events filtered to the v2 GaugeFactory). Pre-launch tokens are priced via hardcoded conversion rates until each token's cutoff timestamp; afterwards DefiLlama spot pricing is used.

HoldersRevenue.Staked-LP Fees And Unstaked-LP Rake: Staked-LP share of swap fees, forwarded to veAERO voters via FeeVotingReward.
HoldersRevenue.External Bribes Revenue: External bribes deposited to BribeVotingReward contracts (NotifyReward events filtered to the v2 GaugeFactory). Pre-launch tokens are priced via hardcoded conversion rates until each token's cutoff timestamp; afterwards DefiLlama spot pricing is used.

SupplySideRevenue.Unstaked-LP Fees: Unstaked-LP pro-rata share of swap fees, claimable directly from the pool.

Revenue.NYM Swap Out Fees: Swap out fees sent to RewardManager for distribution to stakers.

HoldersRevenue.Borrow Fees: Borrow fees distributed to satUSD stability pool and satUSD+ holders.
HoldersRevenue.Redemption Fees: Redemption fees distributed to satUSD stability pool and satUSD+ holders.
HoldersRevenue.NYM Swap In Fees: Swap in fees distributed to debtToken stakers via RewardManager.
HoldersRevenue.NYM Swap Out Fees: Swap out fees distributed to debtToken stakers via RewardManager.

methodology.Fees: Gross borrower interest paid across all Zest V1 reserves, derived by inverting the per-reserve reserve-factor against the protocol's accrued-to-treasury delta (gross = protocol_share * 1e8 / reserve_factor). Prices sourced on-chain via Pyth (STX, BTC, USDC), DIA oracle (USDh), and Stacking DAO stSTX ratio contract.
methodology.Revenue: Protocol's share of borrower interest, computed directly as the daily change in the accrued-to-treasury accumulator per reserve.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Lenders' share of borrower interest, computed as gross interest minus the protocol's accrued-to-treasury delta.

Fees.Borrow Interest: Gross borrower interest accrued across all reserves.

Revenue.Borrow interest to protocol: Protocol's share of borrower interest accrued across all reserves.

ProtocolRevenue.Borrow interest to protocol: Protocol's share of borrower interest accrued across all reserves.

SupplySideRevenue.Borrow interest to lenders: Lenders' share of borrower interest across all reserves.

methodology.Fees: Stability fees accrued on Fathom CDP debt positions (XDC and CGO collateral pools). Reproduces StabilityFeeCollector._collect()'s exact rpow-based math against the on-chain stabilityFeeRate, debtAccumulatedRate, and totalDebtShare snapshots.
methodology.Revenue: 100% of stability-fee income accrues to the Fathom protocol — there are no external lenders in a CDP.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Interest paid by borrowers

Fees.XDC Stability Fees: Per-second stability-fee accrual on outstanding FXD debt backed by XDC collateral.
Fees.CGO Stability Fees: Per-second stability-fee accrual on outstanding FXD debt backed by CGO collateral.

Revenue.XDC Stability Fees To Treasury: Stability-fee revenue from the XDC pool — 100% retained by the Fathom protocol.
Revenue.CGO Stability Fees To Treasury: Stability-fee revenue from the CGO pool — 100% retained by the Fathom protocol.

methodology.Fees: Bonding-curve trade fees paid by users (pump's slice + creator/cashback slice), graduation fees, and Mayhem-mode fees.
methodology.Revenue: Pump's slice of the bonding-curve trade fee plus graduation fees and Mayhem-mode fees.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Pump's slice kept by the protocol after the buyback share, plus 100% of graduation and Mayhem fees. Era-based split: 100% pre-2025-07-14, 0% from 2025-07-14, 50% from 2026-04-28.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: PUMP token buyback (sourced from the fees.pump.fun API; aggregates buybacks across all pump products, so it won't sum exactly with ProtocolRevenue here).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Creator fees paid out to coin creators and cashback returned to traders/holders of the coin.

Fees.Pump Fun Protocol Fees: Pump's slice of the bonding-curve trade fee (1% pre-2025-05-13, 0.95% after).
Fees.Pump Fun Creator Fees: Creator slice of the bonding-curve trade fee (0.05% from 2025-05-13, 0.30% from Project Ascend / Sept 2025).
Fees.Pump Fun Cashback: Cashback Coins slice — paid by traders; same 0.30% slot as the creator fee, mutually exclusive per coin (Cashback Coins from Feb 2026).
Fees.Pump Fun Mayhem Fees: Mayhem-mode fees collected at the Mayhem program fee wallet.
Fees.Pump Fun Graduation Fees: Fees received by Pump.fun fee wallets from the token graduation during bonding-curve to Raydium migrations.

Revenue.Pump Fun Protocol Fees: Pump's slice of the bonding-curve trade fee — kept by the protocol.
Revenue.Pump Fun Graduation Fees: Graduation fees kept by the protocol when tokens migrate from Pump.fun bonding curves to Raydium.

ProtocolRevenue.Pump Fun Protocol Fees: Pump's slice kept by the protocol after the buyback share (100% pre-2025-07-14, 0% from 2025-07-14, 50% from 2026-04-28).
ProtocolRevenue.Pump Fun Graduation Fees: Graduation fees kept by the protocol.

HoldersRevenue.Token Buy Back: PUMP token buyback (sourced from the fees.pump.fun API; aggregates buybacks across all pump products).

SupplySideRevenue.Pump Fun Creator Fees: Creator fees paid out to coin creators.
SupplySideRevenue.Pump Fun Cashback: Cashback returned to traders/holders of the coin (Cashback Coins).

methodology.Fees: Total fees paid, including borrow interest, origination fees, liquidation penalties, plus performance fees (a percentage of earned yield) charged on automated actions and manual front-end actions.
methodology.UserFees: Total fees paid, including borrow interest, origination fees, liquidation penalties, plus performance fees (a percentage of earned yield) charged on automated actions and manual front-end actions.
methodology.Revenue: Treasury share of borrow interest and liquidations, plus full origination fees and performance fees (a percentage of earned yield) from automated and manual front-end actions.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Lender share of borrow interest, plus liquidation penalties distributed to lending pool tranches and keeper bonuses paid to auction initiators/terminators.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Gross protocol-side fees collected before ART (Recovery Token) holder rebates are paid out.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: USDC rebates redeemed by ART (Recovery Token) holders. Funded from prior-epoch fee collections.

Fees.Borrow Interest: Interest paid by borrowers in Arcadia lending pools.
Fees.Origination Fees: Fees charged on the principal of new borrows.
Fees.Liquidation Fees: Liquidation penalties paid by liquidated account owners.
Fees.Keeper Rewards: Initiator and terminator rewards paid to keepers that trigger and finish liquidation auctions.
Fees.Performance Fees: Percentage of earned yield taken on both automated actions (compounding, rebalancing, yield claiming, swaps) and manual front-end actions.

UserFees.Borrow Interest: Interest paid by borrowers in Arcadia lending pools.

Revenue.Liquidation Fees: Share of liquidation penalties routed to the treasury.
Revenue.Performance Fees: Percentage of earned yield taken on both automated actions (compounding, rebalancing, yield claiming, swaps) and manual front-end actions.

SupplySideRevenue.Borrow Interest: Share of borrow interest distributed across lending pool tranches.
SupplySideRevenue.Liquidation Fees: Share of liquidation penalty distributed to the most junior tranche.
SupplySideRevenue.Keeper Rewards: Initiator and terminator rewards paid to keepers running liquidation auctions.

ProtocolRevenue.Borrow Interest: Share of borrow interest routed to the treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Origination Fees: Full origination fee routed to the treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Liquidation Fees: Share of liquidation penalties routed to the treasury.

HoldersRevenue.Recovery Token Redemptions: USDC paid out when ART holders redeem accumulated rebates.

methodology.Fees: Swap fees charged by fly.trade's DexAggregator (a conditional 0.01%-0.1% fee on long-tail assets and specific pairs) plus positive slippage (surplus) retained by the router, taken from each Swap event. Pre-2026-05-03 (router migration) fees are taken from the legacy fee collector.
methodology.Revenue: Swap fees routed to fly.trade's own fee collector, plus the retained positive slippage (surplus).
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Swap fees and retained surplus kept by fly.trade.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Swap fees routed to affiliate / integrator addresses (the referral cut).

SupplySideRevenue.Affiliate Fees: Fees paid out to affiliates / integrators.

methodology.Fees: Total swap fees paid by traders. Per-pool fee rate read from CLPool.fee() (tickSpacing-based default, customizable) applied to each swap's input amount.
methodology.Revenue: veAERO holders' share of swap fees, equal to HoldersRevenue (Aerodrome's zero-leak model routes all protocol revenue to voters).
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Percentage of swap fees going to treasury
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Sum of (a) staked-LP fees and (b) the unstaked-LP rake (CLFactory.getUnstakedFee, default 10% of unstaked share), both routed into the gauge's CLPool.gaugeFees() accumulator. Measured on-chain as gaugeFees(toBlock) - gaugeFees(fromBlock) plus CollectFees event amounts (which drain the accumulator each Voter.distribute call).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Unstaked LPs' net share of swap fees after the rake, accruing via the pool's feeGrowthGlobal. Computed per pool as Fees - HoldersRevenue.

Revenue.Staked-LP Fees And Unstaked-LP Rake: Both flow into the gauge's gaugeFees accumulator and are distributed to veAERO voters via FeeVotingReward.
Revenue.External Bribes Revenue: External bribes deposited to BribeVotingReward contracts (NotifyReward events filtered to slipstream GaugeFactories). Pre-launch tokens are priced via hardcoded conversion rates until each token's cutoff timestamp; afterwards DefiLlama spot pricing is used.

HoldersRevenue.Staked-LP Fees And Unstaked-LP Rake: Both flow into the gauge's gaugeFees accumulator and are distributed to veAERO voters via FeeVotingReward.
HoldersRevenue.External Bribes Revenue: External bribes deposited to BribeVotingReward contracts (NotifyReward events filtered to slipstream GaugeFactories). Pre-launch tokens are priced via hardcoded conversion rates until each token's cutoff timestamp; afterwards DefiLlama spot pricing is used.

SupplySideRevenue.Unstaked-LP Fees: Unstaked LPs' pro-rata share of swap fees, net of the unstaked-LP rake redirected to the gauge.

methodology.UserFees: All fees are paid by traders.
methodology.Fees: Opening/closing fees (percentage of position size, varies by collateral asset and instrument), borrow interest (floating rate on utilized amount), performance fees (percentage of profit on winning Cat Mode/Options trades), funding fees (LP/reserve portion of funding rate payments from the heavier OI side), liquidation fees (remaining collateral after 85% loss threshold), and trigger bot fees. Excludes artificial spread (built into entry price).
methodology.Revenue: Protocol share of open/close fees (1 - lexPartF) plus performance fees on profitable Cat Mode and Options trades.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Protocol share of open/close fees (1 - lexPartF) plus performance fees on profitable Cat Mode and Options trades.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Fees going to governance token holders
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Borrow interest (100% to LexPool LPs), LP share of open/close fees (per-pool lexPartF ratio), funding fees (LP/reserve portion diverted from funding rate payments), liquidation fees (remaining collateral sent to liquidity pool), and trigger fees to keeper bots.
methodology.Volume: Notional value of settled perpetual trades (collateral x leverage). Includes Regular Mode (2-249x), Cat Mode (250-500x), and Up/Down Options.

Fees.Open/Close Fees: Percentage of position size charged on open and close for Regular Mode (2-249x) trades. Fee rate varies by collateral asset and traded instrument. Cat Mode and Options do not pay open/close fees.
Fees.Borrow Interest: Floating borrow rate charged on the utilized amount (collateral x take-profit target) while a position is open. Rate increases with pool utilization and is updated per block.
Fees.Performance Fees: Percentage of profit charged on winning Cat Mode (250-500x) and Up/Down Options trades. 0% on losing trades. A minimum cost applies if the fee is below the platform threshold.
Fees.Trigger Bot Fees: Execution fees paid to keeper bots for limit orders, stop-losses, take-profits, and liquidations.
Fees.Liquidation Fees: When losses reach 85% of collateral (liquidationThresholdF), the position is liquidated and the remaining ~15% of collateral is taken as a fee.
Fees.Funding Fees: LP/reserve portion of funding rate payments. The heavier OI side pays funding; a portion is diverted to the liquidity pool and reserve fund as risk compensation.

Revenue.Protocol Fees: Protocol treasury share of open/close fees (1 - lexPartF).
Revenue.Performance Fees: Performance fees from profitable Cat Mode and Options trades.

SupplySideRevenue.LP Fees: LexPool LP share of open/close fees, set per pool by the on-chain lexPartF parameter.
SupplySideRevenue.Borrow Interest: 100% of borrow interest goes to LexPool LPs as compensation for supplying trading liquidity.
SupplySideRevenue.Trigger Bot Fees: Execution fees paid to keeper bots.
SupplySideRevenue.Liquidation Fees: Remaining collateral (~15%) from liquidated positions, sent to the liquidity pool.
SupplySideRevenue.Funding Fees: LP/reserve portion of funding rate payments diverted to the liquidity pool as risk compensation.

methodology.Fees: On-chain trader-paid fee stream from Extended's trading contract on Starknet, summing per-trade actual_fee values (Trade events) and liquidation fees (Liquidation events). Per Extended's fee schedule, takers pay 0.025% and makers pay 0.000% with a tiered rebate up to 0.013% based on 30-day maker market share. Maker rebates are accrued daily to internal Extended sub-accounts rather than paid out as discrete on-chain transfers, so they cannot be separately attributed from public on-chain data and are not netted from this value. Revenue is therefore left unreported rather than implicitly equated to fees.

methodology.Fees: Fees paid by users using privacy services.
methodology.Revenue: Fees paid by users using privacy services.

methodology.Fees: Redeem fees + yield distribution
methodology.Revenue: 10% of the net profits generated by the protocol strategies and mint/redeem fees
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: 10% of the net profits generated by the protocol strategies and mint/redeem fees

Fees.Assets Yields: The net profits generated by the protocol strategies.
Fees.Mint/Redeem Fees: Users pay a 0.05% fee when redeeming avUSD.
Fees.Performance Fees: 10% of the net profits as performance fees.

methodology.Fees: Gross fees charged on NEAR Intents swaps - the protocol fee plus the affiliate (distribution) fee each frontend collects, summed across all integrators.
methodology.Revenue: NEAR's net captured revenue, measured as NEAR/wNEAR swept into its revenue wallets (reconciles with revenue.near.org and the on-chain buyback). Split by destination wallet into front-end affiliate revenue and Other.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Captured revenue held by the treasury before the 2026-02-23 buyback program.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Since 2026-02-23, NEAR's captured Intents revenue is used to buy back $NEAR on the open market (not burned), returning value to holders.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Gross fees minus NEAR's captured revenue - the affiliate fees kept by solvers and third-party distribution channels (e.g. SwapKit).

Fees.Swap Fees: Gross protocol + affiliate fees charged on NEAR Intents swaps across all integrators.

Revenue.Front-end Affiliate Revenue: Revenue collected by NEAR's front-end fund wallet (near.com / app.near-intents.org).
Revenue.Other Revenue: All other captured revenue: 1Click and partner revenue shares, quote improvement (positive slippage), private agreements, and buyback-wallet flows.

ProtocolRevenue.NEAR Treasury: Captured revenue retained by the treasury before the 2026-02-23 buyback program.

HoldersRevenue.NEAR Buyback: $NEAR bought back on the open market with captured Intents revenue, from 2026-02-23 onward.

SupplySideRevenue.Fees To Solvers & Distribution Channels: Gross fees less NEAR's captured share - kept by solvers and third-party distribution channels.

methodology.Fees: Protocol fees are given by interests paid in credit Tokens by Borrowers to Lenders, cumulated with the amount of SDEX burned at Proposal creation.
methodology.Revenue: Protocol revenue is the total amount of SDEX burned at each new Proposal creation.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Percentage of interest going to treasury
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Interest paid to lenders

methodology.Fees: Token trading and launching fees paid by users.
methodology.Revenue: All fees are revenue.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Revenue portion collected by FastJPEG.

methodology.Fees: Total MEV Tips for Eden Builders

Fees.MEV Rewards: ETH rewards from MEV tips sent to Eden block builders

methodology.Fees: Total yields from deposited assets in all curated vaults.
methodology.Revenue: Yields are collected by curators.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Yields are collected by curators.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Yields are distributed to vaults depositors/investors.

SupplySideRevenue.Euler Yields Distributed To Supliers: Interest yields generated from deposited assets in Euler distributed to suppliers

methodology.Fees: Bribes are distributed to holders, validators, and the protocol.
methodology.Revenue: BGT booster incentives distributed to BGT holders via Berachain Reward Vaults and incentive tokens distributed to validators via IncentivesProcessed events.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: BGT booster incentives distributed to BGT holders via Berachain Reward Vaults.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Incentive tokens distributed to validators via IncentivesProcessed events.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Incentive fee taxes collected by the protocol via IncentiveFeeCollected events.

methodology.Fees: Includes performance fees from profit events, safety-buffer movement, and then the final user yield or loss.
methodology.Revenue: Protocol revenue is only the performance fees.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Protocol revenue is only the performance fees.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: User yield or loss after protocol fees and safety-buffer movement.

Fees.Staking Rewards: User yield after protocol fees and safety-buffer change for the period.
Fees.Performance Fees: Protocol cut from positive YieldAccrued events.
Fees.Safety Buffer For Losses: Change in the safety buffer reserve held by the contract.

Revenue.Performance Fees: Performance fees collected by the protocol.

ProtocolRevenue.Performance Fees: Performance fees collected by the protocol.

SupplySideRevenue.Staking Rewards: Final user yield or loss after fees and safety-buffer movement.
SupplySideRevenue.Safety Buffer For Losses: Safety-buffer increase helps absorb losses; decrease means buffer is released back.

methodology.Fees: All fees paid by users for trading.
methodology.Revenue: Percentage of swap fees going to treasury and/or token holders
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Percentage of swap fees going to treasury
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Money going to governance token holders
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Liquidity providers revenue
methodology.Volume: Total token swap volumes by users.

methodology.Fees: Total interest, redemption fees paid by borrowers and liquidation profit

methodology.Fees: HNT that Helium Mobile bought on the open market (via Jupiter DCA) and burned, funded by subscriber revenue. On 2026-01-02 Helium paused these buybacks and redirected the revenue to network growth, so this is 0 from then on - the subscriber revenue still exists but no longer flows through an on-chain buyback we can measure. We do not count the HNT burned to mint Data Credits for network usage: it is priced at the $0.50/GB protocol peg while carriers actually pay ~$0.10/GB (HIP-149), and that HNT comes from Coinbase treasury wallets rather than open-market buys, so it is neither real revenue nor a genuine buy-and-burn.
methodology.Revenue: Same as Fees.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: 0 - the buyback accrued entirely to HNT holders.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Same as Fees: the buy-and-burn reduces HNT supply, accruing value to holders.

Fees.HNT Buyback: Open-market HNT buy-and-burn by Helium Mobile. Paused 2026-01-02, so 0 from then on.

Revenue.HNT Buyback: Open-market HNT buy-and-burn. Paused 2026-01-02, so 0 from then on.

HoldersRevenue.HNT Buyback: Open-market HNT buy-and-burn; reduces supply for holders. Paused 2026-01-02, so 0 from then on.

methodology.Fees: Total fees paid by users on every swap, determined by the pool's fee tier (e.g., 0.01%, 0.05%, 0.30%, 1.00%).
methodology.Revenue: Protocol revenue represents the share of swap fees diverted to the protocol. This share is set on a per-pool basis and can be updated by governance. Default share is 0%.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Calculated per-pool. feeProtocol is a uint8 containing two uint4 values: feeProtocol0 (lower 4 bits) and feeProtocol1 (upper 4 bits). If feeProtocol0 = x1 and feeProtocol1 = x2, protocol revenue = Total Fees * (1/x1 + 1/x2) / 2. If only one is set, protocol revenue = Total Fees * 1/x.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Money going to governance token holders
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: The portion of swap fees distributed to Liquidity Providers (LPs). This is (Total Fees - Protocol Revenue) for each pool.

methodology.Revenue: Zero by policy. Per Yuzu Money's docs the protocol takes no performance fee on yield; its economic accrual is via the off-chain Reserve Fund surplus/deficit buffer, which is not modelled here.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Zero by policy (see Revenue).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Full vault yield distributed to syzUSD / yzPP / yzPrime depositors via share-rate appreciation.

Fees.syzUSD Staking Yield To Stakers: Daily growth of syzUSD's totalAssets/totalSupply ratio multiplied by today's supply, in yzUSD units.
Fees.yzPP First-Loss Tranche Yield To Holders: Daily growth of yzPP's totalAssets/totalSupply ratio multiplied by today's supply, in USDT0 units. yzPP earns a base yield tracking syzUSD plus a protocol-funded bonus budget from the Reserve Fund.
Fees.yzPrime Yield To Holders: Daily growth of yzPrime's totalAssets/totalSupply ratio multiplied by today's supply. yzPrime is the Yuzu Prime product on Monad with continuous yield distribution.

SupplySideRevenue.syzUSD Staking Yield To Stakers: yzUSD stakers receive 100% of the underlying strategy yield via syzUSD share-rate appreciation.
SupplySideRevenue.yzPP First-Loss Tranche Yield To Holders: yzPP holders receive base yield plus a protocol-funded bonus, in exchange for accepting first-loss risk.
SupplySideRevenue.yzPrime Yield To Holders: yzPrime holders receive the full Yuzu Prime yield via share-rate appreciation.

methodology.Fees: V1 fees are counted from the legacy bonding curve, LP manager, and fee distributor events. Legacy LP manager splits use the initial fee split and subsequent SetConfig event history. V2 fees include protocol fees, creator fees, and NadFunPair LP swap fees. Creator fees are included in total fees but excluded from protocol revenue.
methodology.Revenue: V1 protocol revenue follows the legacy fee accounting. V2 protocol revenue includes actual quote-token transfers to the protocol fee receiver plus the protocol share of NadFunPair LP fees. Creator fees are not counted as protocol revenue.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Protocol revenue is the protocol-controlled share of fees, excluding creator/vault fees.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Supply-side revenue includes creator/vault fees and LP fees retained by liquidity providers.

methodology.Fees: Total fees from both Auction-based Mining and Block-based Mining. Auction: Dutch auction mechanism where price doubles after each bid then decreases to 0 over 1 hour. Block: Players deploy SOL to mine blocks and earn tokens.
methodology.Revenue: Combined protocol revenue from both mining types. AUCTION (after Dec 17, 2025): 16% total - 10% buyback/burn, 3% staking, 2% liquidity, 1% dev. BLOCK: 9% total - 8% buyback/burn, 0.5% staking, 0.5% protocol fee.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Protocol keeps (excludes staking to holders and motherlodes). AUCTION: 13% (10% buyback/burn + 2% liquidity + 1% dev). BLOCK: 8.5% (8% buyback/burn + 0.5% protocol fee).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Returned to participants. AUCTION: 84% to sellers (previous position owners). BLOCK: 91% to players (88% direct + 3% motherlodes when explode).
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Staking rewards for $MACARON token holders. AUCTION: 3%. BLOCK: 0.5%. Combined from both mining mechanisms.

methodology.Fees: Sum of AMM trading fees (Swap.netFwFee), variable-rate borrower interest (AccrueInterest.interest), fixed-rate CT interest claims plus post-expiry treasury FW accrual (Coupon Token events), and liquidation penalties paid to external liquidators.
methodology.Revenue: Protocol share from AMM reserve fees (Swap.netFwToReserve), variable-rate borrow interest via market fee rate, and fixed-rate interest fees (CollectInterestFee) plus post-expiry treasury accrual (TreasuryFwInterestAccrued).
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Protocol share from AMM reserve fees (Swap.netFwToReserve), variable-rate borrow interest via market fee rate, and fixed-rate interest fees (CollectInterestFee) plus post-expiry treasury accrual (TreasuryFwInterestAccrued).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: AMM swap fees after reserve share, variable-rate interest to lenders, fixed-rate CT interest paid to users (RedeemInterest.interestOut), and liquidation penalties paid out to external liquidators.

Fees.Token Swap Fees: Total AMM trading fees from Swap events (netFwFee).
Fees.Borrow Interest: Variable-rate: total interest from AccrueInterest. Fixed-rate: CT addresses from BondToken.CT() for BTs from AMM CreateNewMarket and fixed lending loan tokens; then RedeemInterest, CollectInterestFee, and TreasuryFwInterestAccrued in FW per CT.

Revenue.Token Swap Fees: Protocol reserve share of AMM trading fees from Swap.netFwToReserve.
Revenue.Borrow Interest: Variable-rate: protocol share via market fee. Fixed-rate: CollectInterestFee and TreasuryFwInterestAccrued.

SupplySideRevenue.Token Swap Fees: AMM swap fees distributed outside protocol reserve.
SupplySideRevenue.Borrow Interest: Variable-rate: borrow interest to lenders. Fixed-rate: RedeemInterest to CT holders.
SupplySideRevenue.Liquidation Fees: Liquidation penalty estimated as collateral value seized minus debt repaid, converted via market oracle price.

methodology.Volume: Sum of notional (size × price) across every trade event (open, close, add, reduce) executed on Hit One in the period.
methodology.Fees: Gross fees paid by users: 1% of collateral on position open + 5% of realized profit on profitable closes.
methodology.UserFees: Gross fees paid by users: 1% of collateral on position open + 5% of realized profit on profitable closes.
methodology.Revenue: All the fees are revenue for the protocol.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: All the revenue goes to the protocol.

methodology.UserFees: Fees collected from user trading fees on TxFlow perpetual markets.
methodology.Fees: Fees collected from user trading fees and funding fees on TxFlow perpetual markets.
methodology.Revenue: All the fees are revenue for the protocol.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: All the revenue goes to the protocol.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Fees going to governance token holders
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: LPs revenue
methodology.Volume: Total daily trading volume from all perpetual markets on TxFlow.

Fees.Maker Fees: 0.015% of the trade volume is collected as maker fees.
Fees.Taker Fees: 0.045% of the trade volume is collected as taker fees.

methodology.Fees: Gross swap fees collected across all Streamlock pools.
methodology.Revenue: Net fees kept by the protocol = gross swap fees minus the LP share and minus referral rebates (40-50% of the protocol fee, paid to referrers).
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Net protocol keep, all accrued to the Streamlock treasury (no governance-token holder distribution).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Fees paid out to capital/relationship suppliers: the LP share plus referral rebates.

Fees.Token Swap Fees: Gross swap fees across all pools.

UserFees.Token Swap Fees: Swap fees paid by traders.

Revenue.Swap fees to protocol (net of referral): Protocol fee share after subtracting referral rebates.

ProtocolRevenue.Swap fees to protocol (net of referral): Protocol fee share after subtracting referral rebates, accrued to treasury.

SupplySideRevenue.Swap fees to LPs + referral rebates: LP fee share plus off-chain referral rebates paid to referrers.

methodology.Fees: Gross yield generated by all Alchemix V3 ERC-4626 (MYT) vaults, measured from each vault's share-price (convertToAssets) growth over the measurement period and grossed up by the on-chain performanceFee(), plus realized Alchemist/Transmuter protocol fees transferred to the fee receiver.
methodology.Revenue: MYT performance fees and realized Alchemist/Transmuter protocol fees collected by the Alchemix protocol.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: MYT performance fees and realized Alchemist/Transmuter protocol fees collected by the Alchemix protocol.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Net MYT yield left to depositors/borrowers after performance fees.

Fees.Assets Yields: Gross yield generated by Alchemix V3 MYT vaults, using each vault's on-chain performanceFee().
Fees.Alchemist/Transmuter Protocol Fees: MYT shares transferred from Alchemist/Transmuter contracts to the protocol fee receiver, converted to underlying assets.
Fees.Transmuter Early-Exit Fees: Synthetic tokens transferred from Transmuter contracts to the protocol fee receiver when users exit before maturity.

Revenue.MYT Performance Fees: On-chain MYT performance fee share of gross vault yield.
Revenue.Alchemist/Transmuter Protocol Fees: MYT shares transferred from Alchemist/Transmuter contracts to the protocol fee receiver, converted to underlying assets.
Revenue.Transmuter Early-Exit Fees: Synthetic tokens transferred from Transmuter contracts to the protocol fee receiver when users exit before maturity.

SupplySideRevenue.MYT Yield To Depositors: Net MYT yield left to depositors/borrowers after performance fees.

methodology.Fees: Sum of (a) borrower interest accrued across every Lend reserve, (b) leverage trading fees from LeverageRfqEngine open/close/collateral-swap events, and (c) RFQ liquidation fees from RfqEngine.
methodology.Revenue: Leverage trading fees and RFQ liquidation fees flow to the Flying Tulip treasury via feeCollector and liqFeeCollector. Borrower interest does not retain a protocol cut on chain.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue. Leverage and liquidation fees both end up at 0x1118e1c057211306a40A4d7006C040dbfE1370Cb (treasury).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Borrower interest only. The on chain reserves accumulator collects interest, the protocol then swaps it to FT on the open market via LendEpochSettlerOperator and distributes the FT back to lenders pro rata via PositionsManager.settleEpoch. FT has a fixed total supply so nothing is minted.

Fees.Borrow Interest: Interest paid by borrowers across every reserve, estimated as borrows * IRM.irmSampleAPR(util) * windowSeconds / year.
Fees.Open Leverage Fee: feeAmount field of LeverageRfqEngine.OpenLeverageFilled and OpenLeverageFlashFilled events, denominated in the trade sellToken.
Fees.Close Leverage Fee: feeAmount field of LeverageRfqEngine.CloseLeverageFilled and CloseLeverageFlashFilled events.
Fees.RFQ Liquidation Fee: amount field of RfqEngine.LiquidationFeeCollected events, denominated in the asset being liquidated.

Revenue.Open Leverage Fee: Open-leverage fees retained by the protocol via feeCollector (Flying Tulip treasury).
Revenue.Close Leverage Fee: Close-leverage fees retained by the protocol via feeCollector.
Revenue.Collateral Swap Fee: Collateral-swap fees retained by the protocol via feeCollector.
Revenue.RFQ Liquidation Fee: Liquidation fees retained by the protocol via liqFeeCollector (treasury).

ProtocolRevenue.Open Leverage Fee: Open-leverage fees collected by LeverageRfqEngine, sent to feeCollector (Flying Tulip treasury).
ProtocolRevenue.Close Leverage Fee: Close-leverage fees collected by LeverageRfqEngine, sent to feeCollector.
ProtocolRevenue.Collateral Swap Fee: Collateral-swap fees collected by LeverageRfqEngine, sent to feeCollector.
ProtocolRevenue.RFQ Liquidation Fee: Liquidation fees collected by RfqEngine, sent to liqFeeCollector (treasury).

SupplySideRevenue.Borrow Interest To Lenders: Total borrower interest accrues to the on chain reserves accumulator; the protocol then buys FT on the open market and distributes it to lenders pro rata via PositionsManager.settleEpoch.

methodology.Fees: Yield skimmed from eva vault backing after holder obligations are covered.
methodology.Revenue: eva retains no protocol revenue from skimmed yield.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: eva retains no protocol revenue from skimmed yield.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Skimmed yield is allocated back to users through LP incentives.

Fees.Assets Yields: Yield skimmed from eva vault backing after holder obligations are covered.

SupplySideRevenue.Assets Yields: Skimmed yield allocated back to users through LP incentives.

methodology.Fees: Two streams: (1) 1% service fee pulled atomically by FrenFlow's FeeCollector contract at trade settlement, and (2) Polymarket builder-fee distributions to FrenFlow's builder profile wallet, restricted to a sanctioned sender allowlist (paid in pUSD or USDC.e on a Polymarket-defined cadence).
methodology.UserFees: Service fees only — these are transferFrom'd from the trader's wallet atomically at fill time. Builder fees are excluded because Polymarket pays them on its own settlement cadence, not at the user trade.
methodology.Revenue: Service fees plus Polymarket builder distributions. All flow to FrenFlow treasury / builder wallet. No liquidity providers.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue — 100% of collected fees are retained by the protocol.

Fees.Service Fees: Per-trade 1% service fee on Polymarket trades routed through FrenFlow. Denominated in USDC.e. Tracked from the `FeeCollected` event on the FeeCollector contract `0x95e47CBC5c4D9434412AF44Ade02B33613EDb787`.
Fees.Builder Fees: Polymarket builder-fee distributions to FrenFlow's builder profile wallet `0x58715321c2c6a216d1259f368c34f987a4a26b64`. Tracked as incoming `Transfer` events of pUSD, USDC.e, or USDC (native), restricted to a sanctioned sender allowlist of known Polymarket payout EOAs to avoid counting unrelated inflows as fees.

UserFees.Service Fees: Paid directly from the trader's wallet at fill.

Revenue.Service Fees: 100% of collected service fees flow to the FrenFlow treasury at `0xb9e912e55454Ce284C38ccFED5b7fbbF327E689b`.
Revenue.Builder Fees: 100% of builder distributions are retained by FrenFlow.

ProtocolRevenue.Service Fees: Same as Revenue — fully retained by the protocol.
ProtocolRevenue.Builder Fees: Same as Revenue — fully retained by the protocol.

methodology.Fees: Partner fees earned by telegram wallet through lighter perps integration
methodology.Revenue: Partner fees earned by telegram wallet through lighter perps integration
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Partner fees earned by telegram wallet through lighter perps integration

methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Fees going to treasury
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Fees going to governance token holders
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: LPs revenue

Fees.Liquidation Fees: Fees paid by users from liquidations.

Revenue.Liquidation Fees: Fees paid by users from liquidations.

methodology.Fees: Fees for minting/rebalancing/selling the index, paid by users.
methodology.Revenue: Transaction, deposit & withdrawal fees collected by the protocol.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: All the revenue goes to the protocol
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Fees distributed to the traders & index setters.

methodology.Fees: Native and ZRO fees paid by users for cross-chain messages routed through the LayerZero V1 endpoint, summed from WithdrawNative and WithdrawZRO events on UltraLightNodeV2 contracts across supported chains. Withdrawals lag the actual send() events by the worker/treasury withdrawal cadence; totals converge over time.
methodology.Revenue: ZRO protocol fees moved from UltraLightNodeV2 to the LayerZero V1 Treasury contract via WithdrawZRO. Historically the V1 protocol take rate has been ~0, so this value is typically negligible. Tokens flow to the Foundation treasury and are not burnt by the contract.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: ZRO protocol fees collected by the LayerZero V1 Treasury contract.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Native fees paid out to V1 relayers and oracles via WithdrawNative. Includes a small protocol-native portion when treasury chooses native payment, but in practice the protocol take is ~0 so this is effectively all worker revenue.

Fees.Service Fees: Native fees paid out to relayers, oracles, and treasury via WithdrawNative.
Fees.Protocol Fees: ZRO protocol fees withdrawn from UltraLightNodeV2 by the V1 Treasury contract via WithdrawZRO.

Revenue.Protocol Fees: ZRO protocol fees moved to the V1 Treasury contract.

SupplySideRevenue.Service Fees: Native fees paid out to relayers and oracles.

methodology.Fees: Mint and redeem fees charged by the ftUSD MintAndRedeem engine on every mint/redeem, summed from Minted and Redeemed events.
methodology.Revenue: All ftUSD mint/redeem fees are sent to the Flying Tulip treasury via sweepFees, so revenue equals fees.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue. Fees accumulate in the engine and the owner sweeps them to the treasury (0x1118e1c057211306a40A4d7006C040dbfE1370Cb).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Not applicable. ftUSD does not pay yield to holders directly. Stakers earn separately via sftUSD/EpochRewardsVault, funded by FT bought on the open market with these fees.

Fees.Mint Fee: feeFtUSDAmount field of the MintAndRedeem.Minted event, summed over the day window.
Fees.Redeem Fee: feeFtUSDAmount field of the MintAndRedeem.Redeemed event, summed over the day window.

Revenue.Mint Fee: Mint fees retained by the protocol treasury (no supplier cut).
Revenue.Redeem Fee: Redeem fees retained by the protocol treasury (no supplier cut).

ProtocolRevenue.Mint Fee: Mint fees flowing to the Flying Tulip treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Redeem Fee: Redeem fees flowing to the Flying Tulip treasury.

methodology.Fees: All swap fees collected by Pool Party (24h), fetched from Pool Party's public API on Send Foundation's validator (api-mainnet.cantonwallet.com). Tokens priced the same way as Volume. Phase 2 will populate supplySideRevenue, protocolRevenue, and holdersRevenue once Send's per-swap fee attribution views are available.
methodology.Revenue: Percentage of swap fees going to treasury and/or token holders
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Percentage of swap fees going to treasury
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Money going to governance token holders
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Liquidity providers revenue
methodology.Volume: 24h spot/swap volume across all live Pool Party pools, fetched from Pool Party's public API on Send Foundation's validator (api-mainnet.cantonwallet.com). Raw per-token volume across all swap legs — matches the headline 24h volume displayed on Pool Party's primary UI at cantonwallet.com/pools/. CC (Amulet) priced via canton-network on CoinGecko; cbBTC (CBTC) priced via coinbase-wrapped-btc on CoinGecko; USDCx, USDC.B, FRXUSD.B, and CUSD priced as USDC ($1 stable proxy) since none have a direct CoinGecko listing.

Fees.Management Fees: 1% yearly fee charged on vault assets.
Fees.Instant Redemption Fees: 1% fee paid when users redeem instantly. Standard 4-day redemptions have no fee.

Revenue.Management Fees: Management fees retained by Nerona.

methodology.Fees: Sum of 0.25% LP fees on every Tempo Fee AMM RebalanceSwap. Fees are denominated in the userToken (the TIP-20 the swapper paid in). All TIP-20s on Tempo's official Token List Registry have a coingeckoId, so the pricing layer resolves USD value automatically.
methodology.Revenue: No revenue from swap fees. Everything goes to LPs.

methodology.Fees: Fees extracted per round: 1% admin fee on the total ETH deployed and 10% vault fee on losers pool after admin. Variable effective rate depending on winner/loser ratio.
methodology.Revenue: Includes all extracted fees (1% admin fee + 10% vault fee).
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Includes admin fees (1% of total deployed).
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Vault fee (10% of losers pool after admin) is forwarded to Treasury, then bridged to Ethereum and used by the buyback bot to swap ETH for SpiceETH on Uniswap — 90% burned, 10% bridged back to Arbitrum and distributed to SPICE stakers via Treasury.distributeYield → Staking.

Fees.Vault Fees: The Vault fee (10% of losers pool after admin) is bridged to Ethereum and then used to buyback SPICE, 90% is burned and 10% is distributed to SPICE Stakers in Arbitrum.

Revenue.Vault Fees: The Vault fee (10% of losers pool after admin) is bridged to Ethereum and then used to buyback SPICE, 90% is burned and 10% is distributed to SPICE Stakers in Arbitrum.
Revenue.Admin Fees: 1% admin fee on the total amount of ETH deployed on each round.

ProtocolRevenue.Admin Fees: 1% admin fee on the total amount of ETH deployed on each round.

HoldersRevenue.Vault Fees: The Vault fee (10% of losers pool after admin) is bridged to Ethereum and then used to buyback SPICE, 90% is burned and 10% is distributed to SPICE Stakers in Arbitrum.

methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue: trading fees retained by the Kickstart protocol, excluding the creator share and the cut that goes to Meteora.

methodology.Volume: Total wager amount placed across all 6 on-chain casino game contracts on HyperEVM.
methodology.Fees: Gross gaming revenue (GGR): total wagers minus total payouts. Can be negative on days when players win more than they wager.
methodology.UserFees: Same as Fees — represents the net cost to players.
methodology.Revenue: Same as Fees. All bankroll liquidity is protocol-owned, so 100% of GGR accrues to the protocol.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Fees. The protocol is the sole bankroll liquidity provider.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Zero — there are no external liquidity providers; all bankroll is protocol-owned.

methodology.Volume: Sum of output token amounts from QuoteSettled events emitted by the Deluthium settlement contract.

methodology.ProtocolRevenue: All swap fees are retained by the protocol.

methodology.UserFees: Same as Fees — all swap fees are paid by users.
methodology.Fees: Sum of `fee_amount` parsed from the Anchor `Swap` event payload that the Cube program emits via `emit!()` into program logs. The event encodes both the total swap fee and the protocol's share — see the Anchor IDL at https://github.com/cubee-ee for the exact layout. Fees are accumulated per input-token mint and priced via the DefiLlama price layer.
methodology.Revenue: Sum of `protocol_fee_amount` (the protocol's share of `fee_amount`) parsed from the same Anchor `Swap` event payload.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue — the protocol's share accrues to the Cube protocol fees authority PDA on-chain.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Money going to governance token holders
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: `fee_amount - protocol_fee_amount` per swap — the LPs' share of the swap fee.
methodology.Volume: Sum of `amount_in` parsed directly from on-chain Cube `swap` instructions on Solana, attributed to the input token mint. Computed via Dune SQL over `solana.instruction_calls` filtered by program id and the swap-instruction discriminator.

methodology.Fees: Sum of two SOL streams collected at every round reset on the Jade board: (1) a 1% admin fee on round.total_deployed forwarded to the Jade fee-collector multisig FW4UFt5nDKE2DLVNj979rXjFkjCdmzs9344JetX8hY9P, and (2) the vault portion (~9% of winnings, or 100% of deployed in no-winner rounds) forwarded to the Jade Treasury PDA F4Uwd5sQT8go5r6iiejrVc2iurYSc2dA4RKvX3cLB8e3.
methodology.Revenue: All collected SOL is protocol revenue.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: 1% admin fee on each round's total_deployed, accruing to the Jade fee-collector multisig.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Vault SOL flowing to the Jade Treasury PDA, later spent on JADE buyback-and-burn (90% burned, 10% paid to JADE stakers).

methodology.ProtocolRevenue: 0.008% builder code fees collected by GRIDer from Hyperliquid Perps grid trading.

methodology.Volume: Volume is the sum of tokenOut amounts from SwapExecuted events emitted by the DEXR FeeAggregatorMaster contract on each supported chain (Base, Optimism, MegaETH, BNB Chain, HyperEVM). tokenOut represents the asset received by the user after the swap completes. Note: this counts only swaps that route through the on-chain Master contract; cross-chain bridges and same-chain swaps that fall back to LI.FI's router are not included here (DEXR is a direct LI.FI integrator but those flows accrue separately).
methodology.Fees: DEXR charges a flat 0.3% protocol fee on the input token (tokenIn) of every swap. The feeAmount field of the SwapExecuted event captures this exactly. The same 0.3% rate applies to LI.FI-routed flows but those fees are collected via the LI.FI integrator portal, not on-chain — and are not counted here.
methodology.Revenue: 100% of the 0.3% protocol fee flows to a Gnosis Safe multisig (no token holders, no buyback). The same Safe address (0xD55cE54Ce3e0985867CD57f4266c27a5b060D665) is replicated across all chains. Revenue equals fees.

methodology.ProtocolRevenue: 0 today. The protocol takes 0% until the 500,000 STRI max supply is fully minted; afterwards, 5% of distributions are allocated to protocol operations.

methodology.Fees: USDC payments for DoubleZero Edge shred subscriptions, measured from settled transfers into the shred distribution flow.
methodology.Revenue: 10% of Edge revenue is burned in 2Z for protocol security.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: No protocol revenue
methodology.HoldersRevenue: 10% of Edge revenue is burned in 2Z for protocol security.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: 90% of Edge revenue is distributed: 50% of the remainder to DoubleZero network contributors, 32.5% to shred-originating validators, and 17.5% to protocol client teams.

Fees.Edge Shred Subscriptions: USDC payments for DoubleZero Edge shred subscriptions, measured from settled transfers into the shred distribution flow.

Revenue.Protocol Security Burn: 10% of gross Edge revenue is burned in 2Z for protocol security.

SupplySideRevenue.DoubleZero Network Contributors: 45% of gross Edge revenue, equal to 50% of the post-burn remainder, is distributed to DoubleZero network contributors proportional to contribution.
SupplySideRevenue.Shred-Originating Validators: 29.25% of gross Edge revenue, equal to 32.5% of the post-burn remainder, is distributed to shred-originating validators proportional to leader shreds.
SupplySideRevenue.Protocol Client Teams: 15.75% of gross Edge revenue, equal to 17.5% of the post-burn remainder, is distributed to protocol client teams proportional to shred distribution.

HoldersRevenue.Protocol Security Burn: 10% of gross Edge revenue is burned in 2Z for protocol security.

methodology.Fees: 3F charges two fees on its leveraged RWA positions. The performance fee is taken on the return of the leveraged collateral, after subtracting the cost of the borrowed debt. The management fee is taken on the full value of the RWA collateral, including the leveraged portion, rather than only the user's deposited capital. Fees are accrued and collected when positions are updated, so a single day can include fees that built up over the preceding days or weeks.
methodology.Revenue: Both the management and performance fees are paid to the protocol, so revenue equals the total fees collected.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Both the management and performance fees are paid to the protocol, so protocol revenue equals the total fees collected.

Fees.Management Fees: Time-based management fees charged on the full value of the RWA collateral held by Position Managers, including the leveraged portion.
Fees.Performance Fees: Performance fees charged on the return of the leveraged collateral after subtracting the cost of the borrowed debt, net of management fees.

Revenue.Management Fees: Management fees collected by the protocol fee recipient.
Revenue.Performance Fees: Performance fees collected by the protocol fee recipient.

ProtocolRevenue.Management Fees: Management fees directed to the protocol fee recipient.
ProtocolRevenue.Performance Fees: Performance fees directed to the protocol fee recipient.

methodology.Fees: Gross user-paid spend on render jobs under the Burn-Mint Equilibrium model (RNP-001). Each job is priced in USD; an equivalent USD value of RENDER is burned on the Solana SPL mint by the burn wallet. The on-chain burn represents the 95% node-operator side of gross job spend; gross fees are derived by dividing the burn value by 0.95. The remaining 5% Network Operator service fee is paid to OTOY off-chain per the Foundation's documented fee schedule and is not directly observed in the on-chain burn stream. Legacy Ethereum RNDR (pre-2023-11-02 Solana migration) is out of scope.
methodology.Revenue: Includes 5% service fees paid to OTOY (company that created RENDER) and 95% of the fees that is burnt
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Includes 5% service fees paid to OTOY (company that created RENDER)
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Includes 95% of the fees that is burnt

Fees.Render Job Payments: Gross user-paid USD spend on render jobs under Burn-Mint Equilibrium (RNP-001). Derived from on-chain SPL burns (burn amount ÷ 0.95), since burns represent the 95% node-operator share.

Revenue.Render Job Payments: Gross user-paid USD spend on render jobs, including the 95% burn allocation and the 5% OTOY service fee. Derived from on-chain SPL burns (burn amount ÷ 0.95).

ProtocolRevenue.Service Fees to OTOY: 5% Network Operator service fee paid to OTOY for infrastructure maintenance (derived from burn × 0.05).

HoldersRevenue.Job Payments to $RENDER burn: 95% of gross job fees allocated to $RENDER burn on Solana (observed as SPL burns from the burn wallet).

methodology.Fees: USDC payments received for Mycelia oracle API calls. Each Mycelia API request requires a per-call USDC payment on Base. The adapter tracks USDC Transfer events on Base to two protocol-controlled wallets: (1) the direct settlement wallet for payments via the Mycelia-operated x402 proxy (full gross), and (2) the Orbis marketplace payout wallet, which receives weekly payouts representing 80% of revenue from Orbis-mediated traffic (Orbis retains 20% as a facilitator fee). Transfers where the sender is one of the two Mycelia-controlled wallets are excluded (self-sends and cross-wallet internal transfers).
methodology.Revenue: 100% of fees accrue to the protocol treasury. There is no supply-side, LP, integrator, or token-holder revenue split.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue — all USDC payments accumulate in the protocol-operated receiver wallets.
methodology.UserFees: Same as Fees — every payment is made directly by an end user (human or autonomous agent) per API call.

Fees.Direct settlement: USDC received via the Mycelia-operated TypeScript x402 proxy (myceliasignal-x402-proxy.service), settling directly to 0xD593832Ce9C2B13B192ba50B55dd9AF44e96700d. Represents full gross fees for direct path traffic.
Fees.Orbis marketplace payouts (80% net): USDC received from the Orbis x402 marketplace facilitator at the configured payout address 0xfF51E0339A17f5fcBa6eAf0E06518d0042ac84bf. Represents 80% of customer payments routed through Orbis (the remaining 20% is retained by Orbis as a facilitator fee). Payouts are batched weekly.

Revenue.Direct settlement: USDC received via the Mycelia-operated TypeScript x402 proxy (myceliasignal-x402-proxy.service), settling directly to 0xD593832Ce9C2B13B192ba50B55dd9AF44e96700d. Represents full gross fees for direct path traffic.
Revenue.Orbis marketplace payouts (80% net): USDC received from the Orbis x402 marketplace facilitator at the configured payout address 0xfF51E0339A17f5fcBa6eAf0E06518d0042ac84bf. Represents 80% of customer payments routed through Orbis (the remaining 20% is retained by Orbis as a facilitator fee). Payouts are batched weekly.

methodology.Fees: The 1% admin fee on every HYPE deploy plus the vault fee (10% of the losers' pool, routed to the buyback). The winners' pot (player-to-player) and the $MRCY refining fee (a holder-to-holder redistribution with no protocol cut) are not counted.
methodology.Revenue: All extracted fees: the admin fee accrues to the team treasury and the vault fee accrues to MRCY holders via the buyback. There is no LP/supply-side cut, so Revenue equals Fees.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: The 1% admin fee on HYPE deployed, sent to the dev/ops treasury (team multisig).
methodology.HoldersRevenue: The vault fee (10% of the losers' pool) funds the on-chain buyback — 90% of the bought MRCY is burned and 10% is distributed to stakers.

Fees.Admin Fee: 1% admin fee skimmed from every HYPE deploy.
Fees.Vault Fee: 10% vault fee on the losers' pool, routed to the buyback at round settle.

Revenue.Admin Fee: Admin fee kept by the protocol (team treasury).
Revenue.Vault Fee: Vault fee spent on the MRCY buyback (accrues to holders).

ProtocolRevenue.Admin Fee: 1% admin fee sent to the dev/ops treasury (team multisig).

HoldersRevenue.Vault Fees to $MRCY Stakers: 10% of the 10% vault fee on the losers' pool, routed to the buyback and distribution to stakers at round settle.
HoldersRevenue.Vault Fees to $MRCY Burn: 90% of the 10% vault fee on the losers' pool, routed to the buyback and burn at round settle.

methodology.Volume: Gross pack purchase volume and marketplace sale volume.
methodology.Fees: Net fees from paid gacha pack sales, battle platform fees, and marketplace fees, minus instant pack buyback payouts.
methodology.UserFees: Net fees from paid gacha pack sales, battle platform fees, and marketplace fees, minus instant pack buyback payouts.
methodology.Revenue: Protocol revenue from paid gacha pack sales, battle platform fees, and marketplace fees, net of instant pack buyback payouts.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Protocol revenue from paid gacha pack sales, battle platform fees, and marketplace fees, net of instant pack buyback payouts.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: No supply-side revenue is reported by this adapter.

Fees.Gacha Pack Sales: Paid pack purchases emitted by the GachaPacks contract. Coupon redemptions are excluded.
Fees.Battle Platform Fees: Per-player battle platform fee, counted when the second player joins and the battle buys packs.
Fees.Marketplace Fees: Platform fee from marketplace sales and accepted bids.
Fees.Pack Buyback Payouts: Instant buyback payouts paid to users, subtracted from fees and revenue.

Revenue.Gacha Pack Sales: Paid pack purchases emitted by the GachaPacks contract. Coupon redemptions are excluded.
Revenue.Battle Platform Fees: Per-player battle platform fee, counted when the second player joins and the battle buys packs.
Revenue.Marketplace Fees: Platform fee from marketplace sales and accepted bids.
Revenue.Pack Buyback Payouts: Instant buyback payouts paid to users, subtracted from fees and revenue.

UserFees.Gacha Pack Sales: Paid pack purchases emitted by the GachaPacks contract. Coupon redemptions are excluded.
UserFees.Battle Platform Fees: Per-player battle platform fee, counted when the second player joins and the battle buys packs.
UserFees.Marketplace Fees: Platform fee from marketplace sales and accepted bids.
UserFees.Pack Buyback Payouts: Instant buyback payouts paid to users, subtracted from fees and revenue.

ProtocolRevenue.Gacha Pack Sales: Paid pack purchases emitted by the GachaPacks contract. Coupon redemptions are excluded.
ProtocolRevenue.Battle Platform Fees: Per-player battle platform fee, counted when the second player joins and the battle buys packs.
ProtocolRevenue.Marketplace Fees: Platform fee from marketplace sales and accepted bids.
ProtocolRevenue.Pack Buyback Payouts: Instant buyback payouts paid to users, subtracted from fees and revenue.

methodology.Fees: Free on send.fun and partners, 2% on non-partner swaps.
methodology.Revenue: Protocol fees minus creator fees.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: None. All of send.fun's revenue goes to SEND stakers.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: 100% of send.fun's revenue is paid out to people who stake SEND.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: None. Zero creator fees on the bonding curve.

Fees.Creator Fees: None. Zero creator fees on the bonding curve.
Fees.Token Creation Fee: One-time fee to launch a token.

Revenue.Protocol Fees: Protocol fees paid to SEND stakers.
Revenue.Token Creation Fee: Token-launch fees paid to SEND stakers.

HoldersRevenue.Protocol Fees: Protocol fees paid to SEND stakers.
HoldersRevenue.Token Creation Fee: Token-launch fees paid to SEND stakers.

SupplySideRevenue.Creator Fees: None. Zero creator fees on the bonding curve.

methodology.Fees: Free on send.fun and partners, 0.05% on non-partner swaps.
methodology.Revenue: Protocol fees minus creator fees.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: None. All of send.fun's revenue goes to SEND stakers.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: All of send.fun's revenue is paid out to people who stake SEND.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Creator fees paid to token creators, plus LP fees kept in the pool for liquidity providers.

Fees.LP Fees: No LP fee on the DEX.
Fees.Pool Creation Fee: No pool-creation fees on the DEX.

HoldersRevenue.Pool Creation Fee: Pool-creation fees paid to SEND stakers.

SupplySideRevenue.LP Fees: Swap fees kept in the pool for liquidity providers.

methodology.Fees: Total fees from NFT sales: 2% protocol fee plus creator royalties
methodology.Revenue: Protocol-retained portion of marketplace fees (2% of volume)
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Treasury/protocol retained portion of fees
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Creator royalties paid on each sale
methodology.Volume: Total volume of NFT sales on the Mintpad exchange (TakerBid + TakerAsk + MakerMatch events)

Fees.NFT Sale Fees: 2% protocol fee applied to NFT sales volume from matched orders.
Fees.Creator Royalties: Creator royalties collected on each sale.

methodology.Volume: Gross accepted OKB stakes on FanVibe real World Cup match and champion markets. Rejected/refunded stake attempts are excluded.
methodology.Fees: FanVibe applies a 0.5% protocol fee to accepted stakes.
methodology.Revenue: Protocol revenue equals the 0.5% accepted-stake fee retained by FanVibe.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: 100% of FanVibe protocol revenue is retained by the protocol.

Fees.Stake Fees: Protocol fee charged on accepted FanVibe stakes, equal to 0.5% of accepted stake volume.

Revenue.Stake Fees To Protocol: Protocol fee retained by FanVibe. FanVibe currently reports no supply-side revenue.

ProtocolRevenue.Stake Fees To Protocol: 100% of FanVibe protocol revenue is retained by the protocol.

methodology.Fees: All supported asset inflows to the tinyplace revenue wallet on Solana.
methodology.Revenue: All supported asset inflows to the tinyplace revenue wallet on Solana.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: All supported asset inflows to the tinyplace revenue wallet on Solana are retained as protocol revenue.

Fees.Platform revenue wallet inflows: USDC, CASH, and native SOL inflows to the tinyplace revenue wallet on Solana.

Revenue.Platform revenue wallet inflows: USDC, CASH, and native SOL inflows to the tinyplace revenue wallet on Solana.

ProtocolRevenue.Platform revenue wallet inflows: USDC, CASH, and native SOL inflows to the tinyplace revenue wallet on Solana.

methodology.Fees: All native GHO Social Payment fees on Lens Chain (new posts, comments, reposts, quotes) plus Hey Pro yearly subscription payments.
methodology.UserFees: GHO paid by users for Social Payment interactions and Hey Pro subscriptions on Hey.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Author shares from Social Payment fees paid to content creators on comments, reposts, and quote posts.
methodology.Revenue: Hey treasury shares from Social Payment fees plus full Hey Pro subscription revenue.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: All revenue retained by Hey treasury from Social Payment and Hey Pro subscriptions.

Fees.New Post Fee: GHO fee paid when publishing a new post through Hey Social Payment (0.03 GHO).
Fees.Comment Fee: GHO fee paid when commenting on a post through Hey Social Payment (0.02 GHO).
Fees.Repost Fee: GHO fee paid when reposting through Hey Social Payment (0.01 GHO).
Fees.Quote Post Fee: GHO fee paid when quoting a post through Hey Social Payment (0.03 GHO).
Fees.Hey Pro Basic Subscription: Yearly Hey Pro Basic subscription paid in native GHO (default 1.99 GHO).
Fees.Hey Pro Pro Subscription: Yearly Hey Pro subscription paid in native GHO (default 4.99 GHO).
Fees.Hey Pro Enterprise Subscription: Yearly Hey Pro Enterprise subscription paid in native GHO (default 14.99 GHO).
Fees.Hey Pro Tier Upgrade: GHO paid when upgrading an active Hey Pro subscription to a higher tier.
Fees.Hey Pro Gift Subscription: GHO paid when gifting a Hey Pro subscription to another user.
Fees.Hey Pro Unknown Tier Subscription: Hey Pro subscription paid in native GHO for an unrecognized tier value.

SupplySideRevenue.Comment Fee To Authors: Author share from comment fees paid to the source post author (0.01 GHO).
SupplySideRevenue.Repost Fee To Authors: Author share from repost fees paid to the original post author (0.01 GHO).
SupplySideRevenue.Quote Post Fee To Authors: Author share from quote fees paid to the quoted post author (0.01 GHO).

Revenue.New Post Fee To Hey Treasury: Hey treasury share from new post fees (0.03 GHO).
Revenue.Comment Fee To Hey Treasury: Hey treasury share from comment fees (0.01 GHO).
Revenue.Quote Post Fee To Hey Treasury: Hey treasury share from quote post fees (0.02 GHO).
Revenue.Repost Fee To Hey Treasury: Hey treasury share from repost fees (0 GHO under current fee schedule).
Revenue.Hey Pro Basic Subscription: Full Hey Pro Basic subscription revenue retained by Hey.
Revenue.Hey Pro Pro Subscription: Full Hey Pro subscription revenue retained by Hey.
Revenue.Hey Pro Enterprise Subscription: Full Hey Pro Enterprise subscription revenue retained by Hey.
Revenue.Hey Pro Tier Upgrade: Hey Pro tier upgrade revenue retained by Hey.
Revenue.Hey Pro Gift Subscription: Hey Pro gift subscription revenue retained by Hey.
Revenue.Hey Pro Unknown Tier Subscription: Hey Pro subscription revenue retained by Hey for an unrecognized tier value.

ProtocolRevenue.New Post Fee To Hey Treasury: New post fee revenue allocated to Hey treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Comment Fee To Hey Treasury: Comment fee revenue allocated to Hey treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Quote Post Fee To Hey Treasury: Quote post fee revenue allocated to Hey treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Repost Fee To Hey Treasury: Repost fee revenue allocated to Hey treasury (0 GHO under current fee schedule).
ProtocolRevenue.Hey Pro Basic Subscription: Hey Pro Basic subscription revenue allocated to Hey treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Hey Pro Pro Subscription: Hey Pro subscription revenue allocated to Hey treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Hey Pro Enterprise Subscription: Hey Pro Enterprise subscription revenue allocated to Hey treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Hey Pro Tier Upgrade: Hey Pro tier upgrade revenue allocated to Hey treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Hey Pro Gift Subscription: Hey Pro gift subscription revenue allocated to Hey treasury.
ProtocolRevenue.Hey Pro Unknown Tier Subscription: Hey Pro subscription revenue allocated to Hey treasury for an unrecognized tier value.

methodology.Fees: All protocol fees: bonding-curve swap fees (curveFeeBps of trade ETH), arena battle fees (arenaFeeBps of the pot), token launch fees (flat launchFee per launch) and hook registration fees (flat registrationFee per registration). Rates are the verified on-chain values.
methodology.Revenue: Fees retained by the protocol: total fees minus the creator share of bonding-curve swap fees.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue — fees retained by the protocol.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Creator earnings: the share of bonding-curve swap fees routed to token creators.

Fees.Token Launch Fees: Flat launchFee charged per token launch.
Fees.Hook Registration Fees: Flat registrationFee charged per hook registration.

SupplySideRevenue.Creator Fees: Share of bonding-curve swap fees routed to token creators.

methodology.Fees: Total yield generated by vault strategies, including depositor yield, curator fees and T3tris treasury profit.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Value flowing to capital providers and the third-party vault curator: net depositor yield + curator performance, management, entry and exit fees. The curator is not t3tris.
methodology.Revenue: Earnings sent to the T3tris treasury. Third-party services are also T3tris revenue but are not observable on-chain.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Earnings sent to the T3tris treasury. Third-party services are also T3tris revenue but are not observable on-chain.

Fees.Vault Yield: Gross yield from vault strategies (depositor yield plus curator performance and management fees).
Fees.Entry Fees: Fees charged on deposits, collected by the vault curator.
Fees.Exit Fees: Fees charged on withdrawals, collected by the vault curator.
Fees.T3tris Treasury Profit: Assets transferred to the t3treasury (T3trisProfit events).

SupplySideRevenue.Depositor Yield: Net yield earned by vault depositors after all fees are deducted.
SupplySideRevenue.Curator Performance Fees: Performance fees paid to the third-party vault curator (feeRecipient).
SupplySideRevenue.Curator Management Fees: Management fees paid to the third-party vault curator (feeRecipient).
SupplySideRevenue.Curator Entry Fees: Entry fees paid to the third-party vault curator (feeRecipient).
SupplySideRevenue.Curator Exit Fees: Exit fees paid to the third-party vault curator (feeRecipient).

Revenue.T3tris Treasury Profit: Assets transferred to the t3treasury (T3trisProfit events).

ProtocolRevenue.T3tris Treasury Profit: Assets transferred to the t3treasury (T3trisProfit events).

methodology.Fees: 0.05% integrator fee HypurrQuant earns on routed swaps, counted as transfers from the swap routers/settlers into its fee-recipient wallet (non-swap inflows like lending or reward claims are excluded).
methodology.Revenue: All integrator fees are HypurrQuant protocol revenue.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: All integrator fees are HypurrQuant protocol revenue.
methodology.Volume: Swap volume routed through HypurrQuant, derived from its 0.05% integrator fee (fees / 0.0005). Underlying DEX volume is not double-counted.

methodology.Fees: Borrow interest paid by borrowers on repaid loans, plus loan origination fees and collateral claim fees.
methodology.Revenue: The protocol's share: the repayment fee (10% of interest) it takes from borrow interest, plus all loan origination fees (25% of interest) and collateral claim fees (0.1% of collateral, excluding NFT/RWA).
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: The protocol's share: the repayment fee (10% of interest) it takes from borrow interest, plus all loan origination fees (25% of interest) and collateral claim fees (0.1% of collateral, excluding NFT/RWA).
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: 90% of borrow interest is paid to lenders.

Fees.Borrow Interest: Total interest paid by borrowers over the full term of repaid loans.
Fees.Loan Origination Fees: 25% of estimated interest, charged to the borrower when the loan is created.
Fees.Collateral Claim Fees: 0.1% of collateral value, charged when a lender claims collateral after loan maturity (excludes NFT/RWA collateral).

Revenue.Loan Origination Fees: 25% of estimated interest, charged to the borrower when the loan is created.
Revenue.Loan Repayment Fees: 10% of interest, deducted from the lender's return when the loan is repaid.
Revenue.Collateral Claim Fees: 0.1% of collateral value, charged when a lender claims collateral after loan maturity (excludes NFT/RWA collateral).

ProtocolRevenue.Loan Origination Fees: 25% of estimated interest, charged to the borrower when the loan is created.
ProtocolRevenue.Loan Repayment Fees: 10% of interest, deducted from the lender's return when the loan is repaid.
ProtocolRevenue.Collateral Claim Fees: 0.1% of collateral value, charged when a lender claims collateral after loan maturity (excludes NFT/RWA collateral).

SupplySideRevenue.Borrow Interest to Lenders: 90% of borrow interest is paid to lenders

methodology.Volume: ETH value traded against the bonding curves: ETH entering the curve on buys (ex-fee) plus gross ETH leaving on sells.

methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue — the protocol keeps the full creation fee and its slice of trade fees.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Creator slice of the bonding-curve trade fee. Creator fees are currently disabled, so this is 0 today; it is populated automatically if they are re-enabled.
