ProtocolRevenue.Token Creation Fees To Treasury: The full creation fee is protocol revenue (kept by the Treasury).
ProtocolRevenue.Trading Fees To Protocol: Protocol slice of the bonding-curve trade fees.

SupplySideRevenue.Trading Fees To Creators: Creator slice of the bonding-curve trade fee (currently disabled, so 0).

HoldersRevenue.Burned NEAR: 70% of gas fees burned, benefiting holders through reduced supply (deflation).

methodology.Fees: Includes traffic purchase, preapproval burn, preapproval renew burn, setup burn, dust expire, holding fee, sender change fees
methodology.Revenue: All the collected fees (traffic purchase, preapproval burn, preapproval renew burn, setup burn, dust expire, holding fee, sender change fees) are burnt
methodology.HoldersRevenue: All the collected fees (traffic purchase, preapproval burn, preapproval renew burn, setup burn, dust expire, holding fee, sender change fees) are burned

Fees.Traffic Purchase: Canton tokens burned to purchase synchronizer bandwidth.
Fees.Preapproval Burn: Canton tokens burned to create a standing authorization for receiving CC transfers without being online.
Fees.Preapproval Renew Burn: Canton tokens burned to renew an expiring preapproval before it lapses.
Fees.Setup Burn: Canton tokens burned to onboard an external party onto the network.
Fees.Dust Expire: Near-zero Amulets whose holding fees exceeded their value, garbage-collected by super validators and burned.
Fees.Holding Fee: Per-round fee accrued on Amulet UTXOs, charged upon consumption (zero after CIP-0078, Sep 2025).
Fees.Sender Change Fee: Fee for creating the change Amulet sent back to the sender in a transfer (zero after CIP-0078, Sep 2025).

Revenue.Traffic Purchase: Canton tokens burned to purchase synchronizer bandwidth.
Revenue.Preapproval Burn: Canton tokens burned to create a standing authorization for receiving CC transfers without being online.
Revenue.Preapproval Renew Burn: Canton tokens burned to renew an expiring preapproval before it lapses.
Revenue.Setup Burn: Canton tokens burned to onboard an external party onto the network.
Revenue.Dust Expire: Near-zero Amulets whose holding fees exceeded their value, garbage-collected by super validators and burned.
Revenue.Holding Fee: Per-round fee accrued on Amulet UTXOs, charged upon consumption (zero after CIP-0078, Sep 2025).
Revenue.Sender Change Fee: Fee for creating the change Amulet sent back to the sender in a transfer (zero after CIP-0078, Sep 2025).

HoldersRevenue.Traffic Purchase: Canton tokens burned to purchase synchronizer bandwidth.
HoldersRevenue.Preapproval Burn: Canton tokens burned to create a standing authorization for receiving CC transfers without being online.
HoldersRevenue.Preapproval Renew Burn: Canton tokens burned to renew an expiring preapproval before it lapses.
HoldersRevenue.Setup Burn: Canton tokens burned to onboard an external party onto the network.
HoldersRevenue.Dust Expire: Near-zero Amulets whose holding fees exceeded their value, garbage-collected by super validators and burned.
HoldersRevenue.Holding Fee: Per-round fee accrued on Amulet UTXOs, charged upon consumption (zero after CIP-0078, Sep 2025).
HoldersRevenue.Sender Change Fee: Fee for creating the change Amulet sent back to the sender in a transfer (zero after CIP-0078, Sep 2025).

methodology.Fees: Transaction fees paid in TIA
methodology.Revenue: Burned coins

methodology.Fees: Transaction fees paid by users for executing transactions on the Cosmos network
methodology.Revenue: No revenue
methodology.HoldersRevenue: None of the transaction fees are burnt

methodology.Fees: Fees paid by users for transactions on the CSC chain
methodology.Revenue: No revenue, as transactions fees aren't burnt
methodology.HoldersRevenue: No holdersrevenue, as transactions fees aren't burnt

methodology.Fees: Gas fees paid by users
methodology.Revenue: Burned coins

methodology.Fees: Daily fees are the sum of gas used multiplied by effective gas price across DuckChain transaction receipts.
methodology.Revenue: No protocol or supply-side split is available, so revenue equals daily fees.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: No supply-side revenue share is computed.

Fees.Transaction Gas Fees: DuckChain transaction gas fees paid by users.

Revenue.Transaction Gas Fees: Same amount as transaction gas fees because no protocol cut is separated.

methodology.Fees: Transaction fees (base + priority) paid by users on the Eclipse network.
methodology.Revenue: Burned coins

Fees.Transaction Base Fees: Transaction base fees paid by users.
Fees.Transaction Priority Fees: Transaction priority fees paid by users.

methodology.Fees: Fees paid by GPU service users
methodology.Revenue: No chain revenue

methodology.Fees: Transaction gas fees (in ETH) paid by users on the Fuel Ignition network.
methodology.Revenue: Blob/data-availability fees (in FUEL) for posting Fuel batch data to the DA layer.

methodology.Fees: Transaction fees paid by users for executing transactions on the Gravity network
methodology.Revenue: All the transaction fees paid are burnt
methodology.HoldersRevenue: All the transaction fees paid are burnt

methodology.Fees: Transaction Fees
methodology.Revenue: Transaction Fees + Auction Fees (INJ burned in auctions)
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Transaction Fees + Auction Fees (INJ burned in auctions)

Fees.Transaction Fees: Gas fees paid by users on each transaction, 100% is burned
Fees.Auction Fees: Exchange fees are auctioned off to the highest bidder for INJ, 100% of auction fees are burned. Auction fees are spread evenly across days in between auctions.

Revenue.Transaction Fees: Gas fees paid by users on each transaction, 100% is burned
Revenue.Auction Fees: Exchange fees are auctioned off to the highest bidder for INJ, 100% of auction fees are burned. Auction fees are spread evenly across days in between auctions.

HoldersRevenue.Transaction Fees: Gas fees paid by users on each transaction, 100% is burned
HoldersRevenue.Auction Fees: Exchange fees are auctioned off to the highest bidder for INJ, 100% of auction fees are burned. Auction fees are spread evenly across days in between auctions.

methodology.Fees: Total transaction fees paid on Noble chain
methodology.Revenue: Burned coins
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: All the transaction fees go to validators (100% of fees)

methodology.Fees: Includes yields on all vaults and management fees (0.50% p.a. on total collateral).
methodology.Revenue: Management fees (0.50% p.a.): Advisor 0.10% + Platform 0.20% + Liquidity 0.20%, applied to total collateral value.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Same as Revenue - management fees collected by OpenTrade protocol.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Net yield distributed to vault depositors after protocol fees.

Fees.Assets Yields: Yields on vaults distributed to depositors.
Fees.Management Fees: Management fees (0.50% p.a.) applied to total collateral value.

Revenue.Management Fees: Management fees (0.50% p.a.) applied to total collateral value.

SupplySideRevenue.Assets Yields: Yields on vaults distributed to depositors.

ProtocolRevenue.Management Fees: Management fees going to protocol treasury.

methodology.Fees: Gas fees paid by users
methodology.Revenue: Burned coins

methodology.Fees: Gas fees paid by users
methodology.Revenue: Burned coins

methodology.Fees: Block rewards and transaction fees earned from SOAP merged mining across Ravencoin (KAWPOW), Litecoin/Dogecoin (Scrypt), and Bitcoin Cash (SHA-256) chains. Values are calculated using the token price at the time each block was mined.
methodology.Revenue: Fees going to buyback and burn of quai token.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: Fees going to buyback and burn of quai token

Fees.Ravencoin mining rewards: Block rewards and transaction fees earned from Ravencoin chain.
Fees.Litecoin mining rewards: Block rewards and transaction fees earned from Litecoin chain.
Fees.Dogecoin mining rewards: Block rewards and transaction fees earned from Dogecoin chain.
Fees.Bitcoin Cash mining rewards: Block rewards and transaction fees earned from Bitcoin Cash chain.

Revenue.Token Buy Back: Fees going to buyback and burn of quai token.

HoldersRevenue.Token Buy Back: Fees going to buyback and burn of quai token.

methodology.Fees: fees paid by data requests in SEDA tokens
methodology.Revenue: seda burned for each request
methodology.HoldersRevenue: seda burned for each request

methodology.UserFees: Users pay $0 for transactions. sFUEL (gas token) is obtained free from faucets and has no market value.
methodology.Fees: SKALE uses sFUEL (SKALE Fuel), a gas token distributed FREE via faucets. While transactions consume gas on-chain, sFUEL has $0 monetary value. User fees are $0.
methodology.Revenue: Network revenue comes from SKL token staking subscriptions by dApp developers, not from transaction fees. On-chain transaction fees are $0 as sFUEL is free.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: Protocol revenue is generated through SKL staking model where dApp developers stake tokens for network access. Transaction fees contribute $0 as sFUEL is valueless.
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Validators/nodes are compensated through SKL staking rewards, not transaction fees. On-chain gas fees in sFUEL are $0.
methodology.HoldersRevenue: No sFUEL is burned or distributed as revenue since it has no value. Revenue model is entirely based on SKL token staking.

methodology.Fees: Transaction fees paid by users on Stacks, computed by querying the Allium database.
methodology.Revenue: None. Stacks transaction fees are not burned
methodology.SupplySideRevenue: Stacks transaction fees are paid to miners/block producers.

methodology.Fees: THORChain's native L1 transaction fee (the NativeTransactionFee constant, 0.02 RUNE per native transaction), charged on native THORChain txs.
methodology.Revenue: The native transaction fee accrues entirely to the THORChain Reserve, so revenue equals fees.
methodology.ProtocolRevenue: The native transaction fee accrues entirely to the THORChain Reserve.

Fees.Network Fees: Native L1 transaction fee (0.02 RUNE/tx) paid on native THORChain transactions.

Revenue.Network Fees: Native transaction fee retained by the THORChain Reserve.

ProtocolRevenue.Network Fees: Native transaction fee retained by the THORChain Reserve.
